examples.title: Cache Memory Operation - Write-Through
examples.description: Demonstrates how cache memory works with write-through policy
examples.code:
// Cache Simulation with Write-Through Policy
CONSTANTS
    CACHE_SIZE = 16          // Cache lines
    MEMORY_SIZE = 1024
    BLOCK_SIZE = 4           // Words per block

DATA STRUCTURES
    CACHE_LINE: 
        valid_bit (boolean)
        tag (integer)
        data (array of BLOCK_SIZE)
        
    cache = ARRAY[CACHE_SIZE] of CACHE_LINE
    memory = ARRAY[MEMORY_SIZE] of integer

PROCEDURE read_word(address)
BEGIN
    block_address = address / BLOCK_SIZE
    index = block_address % CACHE_SIZE
    tag = block_address / CACHE_SIZE
    
    // Check if block is in cache
    IF cache[index].valid = TRUE AND cache[index].tag = tag THEN
        // Cache hit!
        offset = address % BLOCK_SIZE
        RETURN cache[index].data[offset]
    ELSE
        // Cache miss - fetch from memory
        memory_block = fetch_from_memory(block_address)
        cache[index].valid = TRUE
        cache[index].tag = tag
        cache[index].data = memory_block
        
        offset = address % BLOCK_SIZE
        RETURN cache[index].data[offset]
    ENDIF
END

PROCEDURE write_word(address, value)
BEGIN
    block_address = address / BLOCK_SIZE
    index = block_address % CACHE_SIZE
    tag = block_address / CACHE_SIZE
    
    // Write-through: write to both cache and memory
    IF cache[index].valid = TRUE AND cache[index].tag = tag THEN
        offset = address % BLOCK_SIZE
        cache[index].data[offset] = value
    ENDIF
    
    // Always write to main memory
    memory[address] = value
END

PROCEDURE fetch_from_memory(block_address)
BEGIN
    // Simulate slower memory access
    start_address = block_address * BLOCK_SIZE
    block_data = memory[start_address to start_address + BLOCK_SIZE - 1]
    RETURN block_data
END
examples.explanation: Shows how cache memory works: on read, checks if data is in cache (hit) or needs to be fetched from memory (miss). Write-through ensures consistency by updating both cache and main memory.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) on cache hit, O(BLOCK_SIZE) on miss
